title: Runfile
description: Schema for Runfile.json — a modern, cross-platform command runner configuration.

$schema.description: Schema version identifier. Use "https://github.com/Skiley/runfile/releases/latest/download/v0.schema.json" during development, or a URL/path to this schema file.

includes.description: Other Runfile.json files to include. Each entry is either a path string (no namespace) or an object with `path` and an optional `namespace`. When a namespace is set, every target name and every `@target` reference inside that included file is prefixed with `<namespace>:`. Children are sealed: a `@target` reference inside an included file resolves to that file's own targets, never to the parent's. Nested includes compose left-to-right.

targets.description: Named targets that can be invoked via the Runfile CLI.

IncludeEntry.description: An entry in the top-level `includes` array. Either a path string (no namespace) or an object with `path` and an optional `namespace`. A blank or omitted namespace is equivalent to the string form.

path.description: Path to the included Runfile, relative to the file that declares it.

namespace.description: Optional prefix applied to every target name, alias, and `@target` reference inside the included file. May not be empty (or omit the field), contain ':' or whitespace, contain '?' (reserved for the `@?target` optional-call marker), or start with '@', ':', or '_'. Nested namespaces compose left-to-right.

CommandStep.description: A step inside a `commands` array. Either a raw shell command string, a target invocation (`@target [args...]` — string starting with `@`, or `@?target [args...]` to silently skip when the target doesn't exist), a `when`-guarded block, or a control-flow block (`if` / `for` / `match` object).

WhenCondition.description: State guard for a step: when does it run relative to whether a prior step has failed?

WhenStep.description: A `when`-guarded block of command steps. Inner commands run only when the target's running state matches `when`.

when.description: State guard for the inner commands. Default: `success`.

commands.description: Steps to run when the gate matches. May be a single shell-command string (sugar for a one-element array) or an array of command steps.

ignoreErrors.description: When true, failures inside this block do not flip the target's failed state.

IfStep.description: Conditional execution. Evaluates `if` against the current substitution context (CLI args, env, loop scope) and runs `then` or `else`.

if.description: Boolean expression. Supports comparisons (==, !=), logical operators (&&, ||, !), parentheses, and substitutions ({{ ARGS.x }}, {{ ENV.X }}, {{ FLAGS.x }}, {{ LOOP.x }}, {{ RUN.os }}, {{ RUN.shell }}). Mixing && and || requires parentheses. Truthiness: only the empty string is falsy.

then.description: Steps executed when the condition is truthy. May be a single shell-command string (sugar for a one-element array) or an array of command steps.

else.description: Steps executed when the condition is falsy. May be a single shell-command string (sugar for a one-element array) or an array of command steps.

ignoreErrors.description: When true, failures inside this block do not flip the run's success state.

when.description: State guard for the entire `if` block. Default: `success`.

ForStep.description: Iterate over a list, glob, or shell command output. Loop variables are referenced as {{ LOOP.<var> }} inside the body.

for.description: Loop variable name. Matches the regex [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*.

in.description: Iterate over an explicit array of strings, or use the magic value "namespaces" to iterate over every namespace prefix declared via `includes` (alphabetically sorted, deduplicated, composed across nested namespaces).

glob.description: Iterate over file paths matching this glob pattern (relative to the working directory).

shell.description: Iterate over the lines of stdout from running this shell command. Lines are trimmed; empty lines are dropped. The iterator command is run at planning time before any iteration body executes.

do.description: Body steps executed once per iteration. May be a single shell-command string (sugar for a one-element array) or an array of command steps.

parallel.description: When true, iterations run concurrently. Inner `for` blocks inside an outer parallel context are forced sequential regardless of this flag.

ignoreErrors.description: When true, failures inside the body do not flip the run's success state.

when.description: State guard for the entire `for` block. Default: `success`.

MatchStep.description: Multi-way dispatch on a substituted value. Resolves the `match` template through the normal substitution pipeline ({{ ARGS.x }}, {{ ENV.X }}, {{ LOOP.x }}, {{ RUN.* }}, chained fallbacks like {{ ARGS.x ? ENV.X ? default }}, etc.), then runs the matching branch by string equality against the `cases` keys. When no case matches and no `default` is set, execution errors out and lists the valid cases.

match.description: The substitution expression whose resolved value selects a case. Supports the full {{ ... }} syntax including chained fallbacks.

cases.description: Map from case value (compared as a string against the resolved match value) to the steps to run. Each value is either a single shell-command string (sugar for a one-element array) or an array of command steps.

default.description: Steps run when no case matches the resolved value. When omitted, an unmatched value is a hard error that lists the valid cases. May be a single shell-command string (sugar for a one-element array) or an array of command steps.

ignoreErrors.description: When true, failures inside the chosen branch do not flip the run's success state.

when.description: State guard for the entire `match` block. Default: `success`.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this target does. Shown in `run :list` output.

aliases.description: Alternative names for this target. Aliases can be used interchangeably with the target name.

commands.description: Command steps to execute sequentially. May be a single shell-command string (sugar for a one-element array) or an array of command steps. Each array entry is either a raw shell command string or a control-flow object (`if` / `for` / `when`).

envFiles.description: File paths to load environment variables from. Supports {{ ARGS.* }} and {{ ENV.* }} substitution. Loaded before the inline `env` object, so `env` values override these. Missing files are silently ignored.

env.description: Environment variables specific to this target. These override global env vars and envFiles.

forceShell.description: Force a specific shell for this target. Accepts a shell name (bash/zsh/sh/fish/powershell/cmd), an absolute path, or a {{ ... }} substitution that resolves to one of those at runtime.

addToPath.description: Directories to prepend to PATH for this target. Relative paths are resolved from the Runfile.json directory.

logging.description: When true, print each command to stderr before executing it.

ignoreErrors.description: When true, continue executing subsequent commands even if one fails, and exit with code 0.

detach.description: When true, spawn the commands as a detached background process and exit immediately. Requires parallel: true when there are multiple commands.

parallel.description: When true, execute all commands in parallel instead of sequentially. All commands are spawned at the same time; the target finishes when all commands have exited.

confirm.description: Prompt message shown before executing. Requires y/N confirmation. Skipped in CI or with --yes.

forceKillOnSigInt.description: When true, forcefully kill the spawned process tree on SIGINT/CTRL+C. Useful for GUI-subsystem apps that don't respond to console signals.

extendStdio.description: Log files to tail during command execution, routing new complete lines to stdout or stderr.

watch.description: Glob patterns for watch mode. When present, the target automatically re-runs on matching file changes. Use ! prefix to exclude.

onlyInDirectories.description: Restrict this target to only be available when the current directory is at or under one of the specified paths (relative to the Runfile location).

Globals.description: Global configuration that applies to all targets. Individual targets can override any of these settings.

addToPath.description: Directories to prepend to PATH before running any command.

envFiles.description: File paths to load environment variables from for all targets. Supports {{ ARGS.* }} and {{ ENV.* }} substitution. Loaded before the inline `env` object. Missing files are silently ignored.

env.description: Environment variables set for all targets. Target-level env vars override these.

forceShell.description: Force all targets to use a specific shell. Can be overridden per-target.

logging.description: When true, print each command to stderr before executing it.

ignoreErrors.description: When true, continue executing subsequent commands even if one fails.

onlyInDirectories.description: Restrict this Runfile to only be available when the current directory is at or under one of the specified paths (relative to the Runfile location). Used with global files.

forceKillOnSigInt.description: When true, forcefully kill the spawned process tree on SIGINT/CTRL+C for all targets. Can be overridden per-target.

WorkingDirectory.description: Working directory for command execution — a free-form path (absolute or relative) that supports {{ ... }} substitution. Defaults to {{ RUN.parent }} (the source Runfile's directory). Useful builtins: {{ RUN.parent }} (the target's source Runfile directory), {{ RUN.cwd }} (the caller's current working directory), {{ RUN.file }} (the source Runfile path itself). Relative paths resolve against the target's source Runfile directory.

ExtendStdio.description: A log file to tail and route to stdout or stderr during command execution.

fromFile.description: Path to the log file to tail. Relative paths are resolved from the working directory.

StdioStream.description: Which standard stream to route log file contents to.
